title: Deliciously Crispy Jamaican Bacon Skillet
description: A spicy and crispy Jamaican dish featuring bacon and wok-hei, perfect for a cozy meal, dairy-free friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the diced potatoes with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until they are golden brown.
While the potatoes are roasting, cook the bacon slices in a large skillet over medium-high heat until crispy, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, chopped onion, and minced garlic, and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the chopped scotch bonnet peppers to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the roasted potatoes to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and pepper mixture.
Crumble the cooked bacon into the skillet and stir to combine with the potato mixture.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot.
